title: creamy granulated malaysian pasta fried-rice
description: a creamy, indulgent twist on traditional fried rice, using malaysian flavors and pasta water emulsion. serves as a satisfying brunch option.
instructions: heat 2 tbsp of butter in a cataplana over medium heat.
add chopped onion and cook until translucent, about 3-4 minutes.
add minced garlic and cook for an additional minute.
add 2 cups of chicken bouillon and bring to a simmer.
stir in 2 tbsp of soy sauce and 1 tbsp of rice vinegar.
add 8 oz of pasta and cook until al dente, about 8-10 minutes.
reserving 1 cup of pasta water, drain the cooked pasta and set aside.
in a blender or food processor, blend together the pasta water, 1 cup of milk, and 2 tbsp of granulated sugar until smooth.
gradually stir the blended mixture into the cataplana with the cooked pasta.
cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly.
add 2 tbsp of unsalted butter and stir until melted.
Cook poultry to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F before serving.
